title: Your Aging Brain Can Still Learn New Tricks
subtitle: 谁说老了就学不动？大脑比你想的更强大

paragraphs.en: Some people have said aging is more a slide into forgetfulness than a journey towards wisdom. However, a growing body of research suggests that late-in-life learning is possible. In reality, education does an aging brain good. Throughout life, people's brains constantly renovate themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 有人说衰老与其说是通往智慧的旅程，不如说是滑向健忘的斜坡。然而，越来越多的研究表明晚年学习是可能的。事实上，教育对老化的大脑有益。终其一生，人的大脑都在不断自我更新。

paragraphs.en: In the late 1960s, British brain scientist Geoffrey Raisman spied growth in damaged brain regions of rats through an electron microscope; their brains were forging new connections. This meant brains may change every time a person learns something new. Of course, that doesn't mean the brain isn't affected by the effects of time.
paragraphs.zh: 20世纪60年代末，英国脑科学家杰弗里·莱斯曼通过电子显微镜观察到老鼠受损脑区出现生长，它们的大脑正在建立新连接。这意味着每当人学习新事物时，大脑都可能发生变化。当然，这并不意味着大脑不受时间影响。

paragraphs.en: Just as height usually declines over the years, so does brain volume: humans lose about 4 percent every decade starting in their 40s. But that reduction doesn't necessarily make people think slower; as long as we are alive and functioning, we can alter our brains with new information and experiences. In fact, scientists now suspect accumulating novel experiences, facts, and skills can keep people's minds more flexible.
paragraphs.zh: 正如身高通常随年龄下降，大脑体积也会减小：人类从40多岁起每十年约缩小4%。但这种缩小未必使人思维变慢；只要活着且正常运转，我们就能用新信息和经历改变大脑。事实上，科学家如今推测，积累新颖的经历、事实和技能能让人的思维更加灵活。

paragraphs.en: New pathways can strengthen our ever-changing mental structure, even as the brain shrinks. Conventional fixes like word puzzles and brain-training apps can contribute to mental durability. Even something as simple as taking a different route to the grocery store or going somewhere new on vacation can keep the brain healthy.
paragraphs.zh: 即便大脑在缩小，新通路也能强化我们不断变化的心智结构。字谜和大脑训练应用等传统方法有助于维持心智耐久性。即便只是换条路线去杂货店或度假时去个新地方这样简单的事，也能保持大脑健康。

paragraphs.en: A desire for new life challenges can further boost brainpower. Research about aging adults who take on new enterprises shows improved function and memory as well as a reduced risk of mental disease. Openness — a characteristic defined by curiosity and a desire for knowledge — may also help folks pass brain tests.
paragraphs.zh: 对新生活挑战的渴望更能进一步提升脑力。关于从事新事业的老年人的研究表明，其功能和记忆力得到改善，罹患精神疾病的风险也降低。开放性——一种由好奇心和求知欲界定的特质——或许也有助于人们通过大脑测试。

paragraphs.en: Some folks are born with this take-in-the-world attitude, but those who aren't as genetically gifted aren't necessarily out of luck. While genes can encourage an interest in doing new things, a 2012 study in the journal Psychology and Aging found completing reasoning tasks like puzzles and number games can enhance that desire for novel experiences, which can, in turn, refresh the brain.
paragraphs.zh: 有些人生来就有这种拥抱世界的态度，但基因上没那么幸运的人未必就没有机会。虽然基因可能激发尝试新事物的兴趣，但《心理学与衰老》期刊2012年的一项研究发现，完成拼图和数字游戏等推理任务能增强对新颖体验的渴望，进而使大脑焕然一新。

paragraphs.en: That's why brain scientist Richard Kennedy says 'It's not that old dogs can't learn new tricks. It's that maybe old dogs don't realize why they should.'
paragraphs.zh: 正因如此，脑科学家理查德·肯尼迪说：“并非老狗学不会新把戏，而是老狗或许不明白为何要学。”
